source_intake_record.raw_question_text:
For black body radiation, Wien's Displacement Law states that its spectral radiance will peak at
$$
\lambda_{\text {peak }}=\frac{b}{T}
$$

where $b=2.89777 \times 10^{-3} \mathrm{mK}$, and $T$ is the temperature of the object. When QiLin tried to reproduce this in a lab, by working with a tungsten-filament lightbulb at $2800 \mathrm{~K}$, he computed a different value for $b$ by measuring the peak wavelength using a spectrometer and multiplying it with the temperature. He hypothesizes that this discrepancy is because tungsten is not an ideal black body. The graph below, courtesy of the CRC Handbook of Chemistry and Physics, shows the emissivity of tungsten at various conditions (the units for wavelength is $\mathrm{nm}$ ).

<image_1>

Assuming QiLin's hypothesis is correct, and assuming there were no other errors in the experiment, how off was his value for $b$ ? Submit $\frac{\left|b_{\text {theory }}-b_{\text {experiment }}\right|}{b_{\text {theory }}}$ as a decimal number, to one significant digit (giving you room to estimate where the points are).
extraction_notes: options is an unlabeled array; mapped by order to A-D
image is referenced as <image_1> and stored as a PIL image object, but no path or URL is provided

image_dependency: 题干显式说“graph below”并要求基于钨的 emissivity 图判断实验偏差，关键信息不在纯文本中。
输入字段 `requires_image=true` 且 `text_dominant=false`，说明该题主要依赖图像内容而非文字本身。
asset_registry_record 中图像存在且可读性指标较高（readability_score=0.8869），支持图像作为主要信息源。
multi_step: 需要结合 Wien 定律、实验设定温度 2800 K、以及图中的钨 emissivity 信息来推断实验测得峰值相关偏差。
题目最终要求计算相对误差 `|b_theory-b_experiment|/b_theory`，至少包含图像读取与公式计算两类步骤。
答案要求以十进制并保留一位有效数字提交，说明不是直接读图即可得到，存在一定推理与计算链条。
verifiability: 答案类型为 `numeric`，且题目给出了明确的目标表达式，便于后续规则化核验。
同时提供了 4 个选项，降低了答案空间，便于自动比对结果。
asset_registry_record 中 `answer_present=true`、`answer_type=number`、`registry_passed=true`，说明样本结构完整且可核验性较强。

decision_reasons: 题干、图像和数值答案均存在，资产完整且 `registry_passed=true`，核心语义支撑充分。
图像依赖度高，但主图文件存在且可读，能够支撑基于图像信息的恢复与求解。
多步推理分和可核验性分均较高，样本质量较好，适合进入候选池。
虽有 `approximate_graph_extraction` 风险，但属于可接受的不确定性，不构成缺失核心语义的理由。

where $b=2.89777 \times 10^{-3} \, \mathrm{mK}$, and $T$ is the temperature of the object. He hypothesizes that this discrepancy is because tungsten is not an ideal black body. The graph below, courtesy of the CRC Handbook of Chemistry and Physics, shows the emissivity of tungsten at various conditions (the units for wavelength are $\mathrm{nm}$).

Assuming QiLin's hypothesis is correct, and assuming there were no other errors in the experiment, how off was his value for $b$? Submit $\frac{\left|b_{\text{theory}}-b_{\text{experiment}}\right|}{b_{\text{theory}}}$ as a decimal number, to one significant digit.

open_ended_problem_variants.title: Dataset Open Question
open_ended_problem_variants.rewritten_question_text:
For black body radiation, Wien's Displacement Law states that its spectral radiance will peak at
$$
\lambda_{\text{peak}}=\frac{b}{T}
$$
where $b=2.89777 \times 10^{-3} \, \mathrm{mK}$, and $T$ is the temperature of the object. When QiLin tried to reproduce this in a lab by working with a tungsten-filament lightbulb at $2800 \, \mathrm{K}$, he computed a different value for $b$ by measuring the peak wavelength using a spectrometer and multiplying it by the temperature. He hypothesizes that this discrepancy is because tungsten is not an ideal black body. The graph below, courtesy of the CRC Handbook of Chemistry and Physics, shows the emissivity of tungsten at various conditions (the units for wavelength are $\mathrm{nm}$).

Assuming QiLin's hypothesis is correct, and assuming there were no other errors in the experiment, how off was his value for $b$? Submit $\frac{\left|b_{\text{theory}}-b_{\text{experiment}}\right|}{b_{\text{theory}}}$ as a decimal number, to one significant digit.
